name: DeepSeek R1

strengths.notes: CONFIRMED across 2 projects with actual output review. R migration review caught 7 critical issues in ggplot2 statistical rendering. Uses a severity taxonomy (INCORRECT/MISLEADING/INCOMPLETE/ENHANCEMENT/CORRECT/OK/MINOR) that makes triage easy. Confirmed CC's pre-identified prediction (geom_smooth confidence band issue). Also validates correct implementations (boxplot quartiles, QQ plots).

Every finding cross-referenced to AP Stats curriculum. Table format includes 'AP Stats Reference' column. Caught mosaic plot (not AP-standard, replace with segmented bar), residual plot x-axis (should be fitted values per AP), dot plot misrepresentation for continuous data.

DeepSeek naturally uses structured severity ratings: INCORRECT (must fix), MISLEADING (produces wrong understanding), INCOMPLETE (missing required element), ENHANCEMENT (nice to have), CORRECT (validated), OK (acceptable), MINOR (low priority). This taxonomy is valuable for the synthesis step — CC can triage by severity.

web_ui_input.staging_strategy: file — supports uploads, handles them well
web_ui_input.notes: CORRECTED — user says DeepSeek handles file uploads and does a better job than Gemini. Previous 'paste only' was wrong.

output_characteristics.format: numbered sections with severity-coded tables: Finding | Severity | Correct Approach | AP Stats Reference
output_characteristics.actionability: very high — exact code for every fix
output_characteristics.style: meticulous auditor — systematic, every chart type covered, nothing skipped

pattern_stability: high — same role, same quality across Python formulas and R/ggplot2 rendering
